summary: Chin and Michelen (2026) established the first vanishing upper bound $P_n \leq n^{-1/17}$, proving that random instances are almost surely unsolvable for large $n$ and confirming a 1989 conjecture of Gusfield and Irving. However, the stronger Mertens conjecture $P_n = \Theta(n^{-1/4})$ remains open; the current best bounds are $\Omega(n^{-1/2}) \leq P_n \leq n^{-1/17}$, leaving a gap around the conjectured exponent $1/4$.

since_posted.title: Stable Roommates Problem with Random Preferences
since_posted.claim: Introduces an $O(n^{3/2})$ average-case algorithm and provides large-scale simulation evidence (up to $n=20000$) supporting the conjecture $p_n = \Theta(n^{-1/4})$.

since_posted.title: On random stable partitions
since_posted.claim: Shows the expected number of stable partitions with odd cycles grows as $n^{1/4}$ (consistent with the conjecture), but with standard deviation of order $n^{3/8}$, too large to conclude odd cycles exist with high probability, leaving the conjecture unresolved.

since_posted.title: The random stable roommates problem typically has no solution
since_posted.claim: Proves the first vanishing upper bound $P_n \leq n^{-1/17}$ for sufficiently large $n$, confirming the Gusfield–Irving (1989) conjecture that $P_n \to 0$, but the Mertens conjecture $P_n = \Theta(n^{-1/4})$ remains open.

since_posted.title: Perspectives on Unsolvability in the Roommates Problem
since_posted.claim: Empirical study of solvability probability across multiple preference distributions (instances up to $n=5001$) and structural analysis of unstable configurations, finding solution sets are typically small.

notes: The Mertens conjecture $P_n = \Theta(n^{-1/4})$ posted to OPG in 2008 remains open. The Chin–Michelen 2026 result ($P_n \leq n^{-1/17}$) is a landmark improvement over the previous constant upper bound $\sqrt{e}/2$, but does not yet achieve the conjectured exponent. The Pittel (2017) result on expected number of stable partitions is suggestive but not conclusive. The lower bound $P_n \geq C n^{-1/2}$ (Pittel 1993, predating OPG posting) has not been improved.
